text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should know that making sourdough starter is a lot like raising a baby. It needs a lot of attention and care. But once you get it going, it's pretty easy. In Star Wars, the Force is an energy field created by all living things that passes through us throughout the universe. It surrounds us, penetrates us, and binds the galaxy together. The Force allows for telekinesis, levitation, and so many other abilities. It is also what gives Jedi and Sith their powers. Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that starter. You want to make sure it's really active and bubbly before you start your dough. If it's not ready, your bread won't rise. Once you've mixed and shaped your dough, you need to let it rise in a warm, dry place. This is called proofing, and it's crucial for the final texture. After the dough has doubled in size, you're ready to bake. Just bake until it has a golden brown crust.
